content.summary: Freed Inc. (freed.ai / getfreed.ai) is a San Francisco-based AI health technology company founded in 2023 by Erez Druk and Andrey Bannikov. The company builds AI-powered clinical documentation and practice management tools for independent and small/midsized medical practices, with its flagship AI Scribe product turning patient visit conversations into structured clinical notes in under 60 seconds. The platform has expanded to include a Coding Assistant (ICD-10/CPT), clinical decision support, a Front Desk AI receptionist, and direct EHR integration via a Chrome extension. As of July 2026, Freed serves 26,000+ clinicians across 1,300+ clinics, transcribed 32.5 million patient visits in 2025, and reports ~$19M ARR (March 2025, growing 134% y/y per Sacra). The company raised a $30M Series A in March 2025 led by Sequoia Capital (total ~$40M raised), and has grown from ~50 to ~146 employees since funding. Key decision-makers are CEO/Co-Founder Erez Druk (LinkedIn: drukerez), CTO/Co-Founder Andrey Bannikov, and Chief of Staff Michelle Burchenal. The tech stack centers on a Webflow-hosted website, a Chrome extension for EHR integration, iOS/Android apps, Stripe for billing, Drata for compliance, and an LLM-based ambient AI inference pipeline. Major buying signals include the active post-Series A growth phase, rapid product expansion into new clinical workflow categories, aggressive hiring, and an explicit platform strategy targeting independent clinics across all specialties.
content.industry: Health Technology / AI Medical Scribe / Hospitals and Health Care
tech_stack: LLM-based ambient audio transcription and note generation (AI core)
content.headquarters: 548 Market Street, PMB 95301, San Francisco, CA 94104
content.total_funding: $40.3M total (~$10.3M across Seed rounds + $30M Series A, March 2025)
buying_signals: Recent Series A funding: $30M closed March 2025 led by Sequoia Capital — actively deploying capital for product and team growth
Rapid headcount growth: ~50 employees at Series A (March 2025) → 146 on LinkedIn (July 2026); opened new SF office with 65+ seats
Product expansion: Launched Coding Assistant (ICD-10/CPT/E/M), Front Desk (AI receptionist), and EHR Chrome extension within months of funding
Strong ARR growth: ~$19M ARR in March 2025, up 134% year-over-year (Sacra estimate)
User base growth: 26,000+ clinicians and 1,300+ clinics on platform; 32.5 million patient visits transcribed in 2025
Named to CB Insights Digital Health 50 (2025) — increased enterprise visibility and inbound deal flow
Platform strategy shift: Expanding from single-product scribe to full clinical AI platform (scribe + coding + front desk + decision support)
SMB-first GTM: Targets independent and small/mid-sized practices (47% of US clinicians), creating partnership and channel opportunities
Actively hiring across engineering, sales, and operations roles
content.headcount_estimate: ~146 employees (LinkedIn, July 2026); LinkedIn bracket: 51–200 employees; ~50 staff at time of Series A close (March 2025)
